paragraphs: You have an Egyptian toe form with medium-width forefoot and a very wide heel. A mixed profile; forefoot and heel require different fits.

Beyond the obvious: Given your long arch, the ball of your foot may be pushed into the toe box. A squeezed forefoot may be caused by this instead of actual shoe width, so look for rather short toe boxes. Additionally your instep is particularly high, so an adjustable closure is preferable to actually get into the shoe. Ideally double velcro or laces, you may struggle getting into slippers if adequately downsized. Your shallow heel doesn't project as far back as most. Deeply sculpted cups will feel empty at the back. Your big toe drifts slightly inwards (could be a mild hallux valgus); we avoid very asymmetric lasts and prefer moderately asymmetric or wider-tipped designs that help the big toe stay in contact with the shoe tip.
interpretation.title: Your Foot Shape

paragraphs: Your La Sportiva Finale Women's (EU 41, at street size) is very relaxed for La Sportiva, where the usual downsize is 2.5 sizes.
Your La Sportiva Finale Women's's roomy toes and loose forefoot are most likely a sizing problem, you could try a size smaller for a better fit.
interpretation.title: What Your Current Shoe Fit Tells Us

paragraphs: Based on your scan and current shoe fit, we target shoes with Egyptian toe form, normal forefoot width, and wide heel width.

Given your preference for sport climbing outdoors on sandstone and a balanced fit, we prioritize balanced-stiffness velcros or lace-ups with slight downturn and no asymmetry (shifted lower to accommodate your mild hallux valgus).
Note: data on shoe heel depth is unfortunately not widely available. We're working on extracting this data from shoe scans, but for now the recommendations match heel width only.
The softer, stiffer, and budget tiers below offer alternative options around this target.
interpretation.title: What to Look For

recommendations.description: Lace-up with Greek toe form, medium forefoot and a wide heel cup. 4.5mm Red Chili RX-2 Techgrip rubber with a full, medium-hard midsole, resulting in a medium-firm shoe with slight downturn and slight asymmetry.
recommendations.tradeoffs: Tradeoffs: Greek toe form might not be a perfect fit for your Egyptian toe, and slightly more asymmetric and slightly stiffer than we target.

recommendations.description: Velcro with Egyptian toe form, medium fit throughout. 4mm Vibram XS Grip rubber with a full, medium midsole, resulting in a balanced-stiffness shoe with slight downturn and slight asymmetry.
recommendations.tradeoffs: Tradeoff: Slightly tighter in the heel and slightly more asymmetric than we target.
